content:
## Purpose and Scope

The Core Information Standard Version 2.0 represents a foundational framework for capturing and sharing essential person-centered information across health and social care settings in the UK. This standard defines the minimum dataset required to support safe, effective care delivery while promoting interoperability between diverse care systems.

## Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, this standard addresses the critical need for consistent, comprehensive information capture across care transitions. It encompasses demographic details, clinical information, care planning elements, and person-centered preferences to ensure holistic care delivery. The standard aligns with UK regulatory requirements including the Equality Act 2010, Mental Capacity Act 2005, and NHS data standards.
title: Introduction

content:
## Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person Demographics Capture | Capture complete demographic information including NHS number, name variations, and contact preferences | NHS Data Dictionary | Mandatory |
| Reasonable Adjustments | Document all reasonable adjustments required under Equality Act 2010 | Equality Act 2010 | Mandatory |
| Mental Capacity Assessment | Record mental capacity assessments and best interest decisions | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | Required |
| Safeguarding Concerns | Flag and document any safeguarding issues | NHS Safeguarding Standards | Required |
| Care Planning | Develop person-centered care plans with clear goals and actions | NICE Guidelines | Required |
| End of Life Preferences | Document CPR decisions and preferred place of care/death | ReSPECT Process | Required |

These recommendations ensure comprehensive information capture to support safe, person-centered care delivery across all settings.
title: Health Interventions & Recommendations

content:
## User Personas

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | Clinical Professional | Healthcare provider (doctor, nurse, therapist) delivering direct care | Clinical assessment, care planning, documentation |
| CISV2.P2 | Social Care Worker | Professional providing social care support | Needs assessment, safeguarding, support planning |
| CISV2.P3 | Administrative Staff | Staff managing patient records and appointments | Data entry, appointment scheduling, record management |
| CISV2.P4 | Person/Patient | Individual receiving care services | Self-advocacy, providing personal information |
| CISV2.P5 | Carer/Family Member | Person supporting individual receiving care | Advocacy, providing collateral information |
| CISV2.P6 | Information Governance Lead | Professional ensuring data quality and compliance | Data standards, privacy, security |

These personas represent the primary users who will interact with systems implementing the Core Information Standard.
title: Generic Personas

content:
## Scenario 1: Hospital Admission

A person presents to A&E requiring admission. The clinical team needs immediate access to essential information including allergies, current medications, and any advance decisions.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Data Required | Demographics, alerts, allergies, medications, legal information |
| Personas Involved | CISV2.P1 (Clinical Professional), CISV2.P4 (Person/Patient) |
| Related Processes | CISV2.BP1 (Emergency Admission) |

## Scenario 2: Social Care Assessment

A social worker conducts a comprehensive needs assessment for community support services.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Data Required | About me, individual requirements, social context, care preferences |
| Personas Involved | CISV2.P2 (Social Care Worker), CISV2.P4 (Person/Patient), CISV2.P5 (Carer) |
| Related Processes | CISV2.BP2 (Care Assessment) |

## Scenario 3: Care Transition

Transferring care between hospital and community settings requires comprehensive information exchange.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Data Required | Full record including care plans, medications, support needs |
| Personas Involved | CISV2.P1, CISV2.P2, CISV2.P3 |
| Related Processes | CISV2.BP3 (Care Transfer) |
title: User Scenarios

content: Three critical workflows support the implementation of the Core Information Standard: emergency admission, care assessment, and care transfer processes.

activities.objectives: Rapidly capture and access critical information during emergency admission
activities.title: Emergency Admission Process

activities.objectives: Conduct comprehensive assessment of care and support needs
activities.title: Care Assessment Process

activities.objectives: Ensure safe transfer of care between settings with complete information handover
activities.title: Care Transfer Process

title: Business Processes & Workflows

content:
## Person Demographics

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | NHS Number | Unique NHS identifier | string[10] | M | NHS_DD |
| CISV2.DE2 | Person First Name | Given name(s) including middle | string[35] | M | NHS_DD |
| CISV2.DE3 | Person Family Name | Surname | string[35] | M | NHS_DD |
| CISV2.DE4 | Date of Birth | Birth date | date | M | NHS_DD |
| CISV2.DE5 | Address Line 1 | Primary address line | string[35] | M | NHS_DD |
| CISV2.DE6 | Postcode | Postal code | string[8] | R | NHS_DD |

## Clinical Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE7 | Allergies | Causative agents | coded/text | M | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE8 | Current Medications | Active medication list | coded/text | R | dm+d |
| CISV2.DE9 | Problem List | Active problems | coded/text | M | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE10 | Mental Capacity | Capacity assessment status | text | R | Free text |

## Individual Requirements

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE11 | Reasonable Adjustments | Required adjustments | coded/text | R | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE12 | Communication Needs | How person communicates | text | R | Free text |
| CISV2.DE13 | Mobility Needs | Mobility requirements | coded/text | R | SNOMED CT |
title: Core Data Elements

content:
## Mental Capacity Assessment Decision

**Purpose**: Determine appropriate consent pathway based on capacity assessment

| Condition | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Person has capacity for specific decision | Obtain direct consent from person |
| Person lacks capacity + Valid LPA exists | Consult LPA for decision |
| Person lacks capacity + No LPA | Follow best interests process with MDT |
| Emergency + Unable to assess | Act in best interests, document rationale |

## Reasonable Adjustments Requirement

**Purpose**: Identify when reasonable adjustments must be provided

| Condition | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Communication impairment identified | Provide appropriate communication support |
| Physical disability affecting access | Ensure accessible facilities/equipment |
| Learning disability | Provide easy-read materials, allow extra time |
| Sensory impairment | Provide appropriate format (large print, audio, BSL) |

## Safeguarding Referral Decision

**Purpose**: Determine when safeguarding referral required

| Condition | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Current abuse/neglect suspected | Immediate safeguarding referral |
| Historical abuse disclosed | Assess current risk, consider referral |
| Self-neglect indicators | Adult safeguarding assessment |
| Child at risk identified | Children's services referral |
title: Decision-Support Logic

content:
## Quality and Compliance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND1 | Demographics Completeness | Records with all mandatory demographics | Total records | By organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND2 | Reasonable Adjustments Flagged | Persons with adjustments documented | Persons with disabilities | By adjustment type | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND3 | Mental Capacity Documented | Assessments completed where indicated | Persons requiring assessment | By decision type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND4 | Care Plan Currency | Care plans reviewed within timeframe | Total active care plans | By service | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND5 | Consent Documentation | Explicit consent recorded | Information sharing instances | By purpose | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND6 | Safeguarding Timeliness | Referrals within 24 hours | Safeguarding concerns raised | By risk level | Weekly |

These indicators support monitoring of standard implementation and identify areas for improvement.
title: Indicators & Performance Metrics

content:
## Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR1 | Demographics Capture | System SHALL capture all mandatory person demographics | High |
| CISV2.FR2 | Consent Management | System SHALL record and enforce consent decisions | High |
| CISV2.FR3 | Audit Trail | System SHALL maintain complete audit trail of all changes | High |
| CISV2.FR4 | Integration | System SHALL integrate with PDS and spine services | High |
| CISV2.FR5 | Care Planning | System SHALL support collaborative care planning | Medium |
| CISV2.FR6 | Document Management | System SHALL store and retrieve associated documents | Medium |

## Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR1 | Availability | System SHALL maintain 99.5% uptime | High |
| CISV2.NFR2 | Response Time | Page load time SHALL be <2 seconds | High |
| CISV2.NFR3 | Security | System SHALL comply with NHS Data Security Standards | High |
| CISV2.NFR4 | Accessibility | System SHALL meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards | High |
| CISV2.NFR5 | Scalability | System SHALL support 10,000 concurrent users | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR6 | Interoperability | System SHALL use FHIR R4 UK Core | High |
title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

content:
## Standards and Specifications

1. NHS Data Dictionary. Available at: https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/
2. UK Core FHIR Implementation Guide. Available at: https://simplifier.net/ukcore
3. SNOMED CT UK Edition. Available at: https://termbrowser.nhs.uk/
4. dm+d - Dictionary of Medicines and Devices. Available at: https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/dmd

## Legislation and Guidance

5. Equality Act 2010. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents
6. Mental Capacity Act 2005. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents
7. Data Protection Act 2018. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2018/12/contents
8. Care Act 2014. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2014/23/contents

## Clinical Guidelines

9. NICE Guidelines for Person-Centred Care. Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/
10. ReSPECT Process. Available at: https://www.resus.org.uk/respect
11. NHS Safeguarding Accountability Framework 2022
12. Professional Records Standards Body Standards. Available at: https://theprsb.org/
title: References
